name: First Mexican Republic (1824–1864)
description: Three vertical stripes — green, white, red — with the Mexican eagle (perched on a cactus, holding a serpent) crowned with a laurel/oak wreath in the centre.
history: The flag of the First Mexican Republic, adopted on 14 April 1823 with refinements through 1824. It established the green-white-red tricolour and the eagle-snake-cactus emblem that have remained the basis of the Mexican flag through subsequent governments. The 1824 version's eagle wears a republican crown of laurel.

features.role: Mexican eagle (perched on cactus, holding serpent)
